message:
fix(ci): cross-platform binary_hash and Windows compilation

- Add Mach-O universal (fat) binary support to binary_hash.rs
  - Parse fat32 and fat64 formats to extract architecture slices
  - Select native architecture slice or fallback to first available
  - Fixes macOS test failures with "Unsupported file format"

- Move essential dependencies from Unix-only to cross-platform
  - anyhow, chrono, blake3, object, shell-escape, etc.
  - Keeps only openssh and whoami as Unix-only
  - Fixes Windows --no-default-features compilation (202 errors)

- Update test_binary_contains_marker_found for platform-specific markers
  - ELF marker on Linux, __TEXT segment on macOS, PE on Windows

- Add pre-build step to coverage job for daemon lifecycle tests
  - Tests need target/debug/rchd binary to be present

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -403,10 +403,12 @@ jobs:
       - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
         with:
           cache-on-failure: true
+      - name: Build binaries for E2E tests
+        run: cargo build --workspace
       - name: Generate LCOV coverage
         env:
           RCH_MOCK_SSH: '1'
-        # Exclude true-e2e feature which requires pre-built binaries
+        # Build debug binaries first for E2E tests that need them
         run: cargo llvm-cov --features rich-ui --workspace --lcov --output-path lcov.info
       - name: Generate HTML coverage report
         env:
